question:
Will Roasso Kumamoto win on 2025-11-08?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for November 8, 2025
If Roasso Kumamoto wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.jleague.jp/en/

evidences.title: Roasso Kumamoto squad and injuries
evidences.content: Roasso Kumamoto squad includes forwards Masato Handai, Jeong-Min Bae, Yuto Katori, and Ora Ishihara; midfielders Shuhei Kamimura, Koya Fujii, Eitaro Matsuda, Ryotaro Onishi, Shohei Mishima, Keita Kobayashi, Akira Iihoshi, Sota Nagai, Rearu Watanabe, Keita Negishi, Kenichi Nasu, and Yuki Yoshioka; defenders Wataru Iwashita, Thae-ha Ri, Yuki Omoto, Kohei Kuroki, Shia Nagashima, Rei Yakushida, Shumpei Toda, and Soma Ito; and goalkeepers Shibuki Sato, Hiromu Musha, Haruki Nishimura, and Yuya Sato. The injuries list includes Yuki Yoshioka and Hiromu Musha with cruciate ligament injuries.

evidences.title: Roasso Kumamoto Season Guide 2025 | FTN
evidences.content: Roasso Kumamoto finished 12th and full 12 points ahead of the bottom 3 last year. It wasn’t an easy campaign for the Stallions though. They never moved into the top 10 and spent 10 weeks inside the relegation zone. The 16-year-old prodigy Keito Kumashiro became the youngest ever goalscorer in Japanese professional football. He ended up with 5 league goals and now a year older many see Kumashiro as a potential breakthrough superstar. Considering Roasso’s bad financial situation and inability to sign players Kumashiro could become a regular starter at some stage. Especially, with the top goalscorer from last year Daichi Ishikawa (10 goals) now gone. [...] Roasso’s big issues were in defence in 2024. They had the equal second-worst record in J2 with 62 goals conceded in 38 games. To make matters worse the main goalie Ryuga Tashiro and one of the few decent defenders last season Yamato Okada are no longer in the squad. The bad finances mean Roasso couldn’t sign proper reinforcements and most of their new arrivals are coming from universities or high schools. The only new signing that could realistically hope for meaningful minutes is the loanee Yutaro Hakamata. [...] ### Out Daichi...

evidences.title: [Match Preview] 2025 Meiji Yasuda J2 League Matchweek 18: Iwaki FC vs. Roasso Kumamoto｜いわきFC
evidences.content: Rosso Kumamoto competed in the Kyushu Soccer League in 2005 and won. They finished 3rd in the 29th All Japan Regional Football Champions League and were promoted to the JFL. In 2006, they were approved as a J.League associate member club and entered the J2 League in 2008. At the same time, they changed their name to the current "Roasso Kumamoto." In J2, they typically finished in the middle to lower ranks. However, they were relegated to J3 after finishing 21st in 2018. They returned to J2 after winning the title in their third season, 2021. Then, in the following year, 2022, the team led by manager Takeshi Oki achieved a breakthrough. [...] Alouette Kumamoto originated from the "Nippon Telegraph and Telephone Public Corporation Kumamoto Soccer Club," founded in 1969. They won the Kyushu Soccer League five times in the 1990s and joined the JFL in 2001 as NTT West Japan Kumamoto. In 2002, they transitioned into a club team and competed in the Kyushu Soccer League as "Alouette Kumamoto." In 2004, the "Headquarters for the Promotion of a J.League Team in Kumamoto" was established, centered around the Kumamoto Football Association. Alouette Kumamoto was selected as the core for the...

evidences.title: The 2025 Market Report – J2 League – J. League Regista
evidences.content: Roasso Kumamoto are playing a wonderful brand of football under wizard Takeshi Oki, who made many players a favour with his 3-3-1-3. Roasso just lost Daichi Ishikawa to JEF United Chiba, and they needed a replacement. Shiohama has been a talisman with FUFC, the clear general of their offensive efforts. Let alone what he could do under Oki – we wouldn’t be surprised to see him stepping up to J1 in 2026. #### 2. Gabriel | Yokohama FC » RB Omiya Ardija [...] FC Imabari have got their promotion, and they surely have already a massive weapon in Marcus Vinicius, but you could never have enough firepower up front. Wesley Tanque scored 6 goals in 16 games in last season’s J3, but he’s kind of a unproven commodity. Another double-digits season by Fujioka would help having a quiet season. #### 3. Ryo Shiohama | Fukushima United FC » Roasso Kumamoto In the Market Report section, when we last talked of possible leaps, Ryo Shiohama was among those names, and it’s even more fitting that he picked Roasso Kumamoto as his next destination. After Fukushima United FC dreamt of reaching J2 – got into the play-offs and drew against FC Osaka -, it was natural to move up the ladder at 24 years old. An...

evidences.title: Roasso Kumamoto to aim for J1 promotion against Kyoto Sanga F.C.
evidences.content: Roasso booked their spot in the match after advancing out of the Meiji Yasuda J2 League promotion play-off. They finished in fourth place in the table to make the four-team promotion tournament, then played to a pair of 2-2 draws in the play-off. Their first draw came against fifth-place Oita Trinita and the second against sixth-place Montedio Yamagata, with Roasso advancing on both occasions thanks to their higher seeding. Roasso’s rise has been remarkable. They have never played in J1 before, and were down in the fourth tier as recently as 2005, but after being promoted to J2 for 2022, they put together a season to remember and are now on the verge of making the leap to the top flight for the first time ever.
